verdict_reasoning: FN1 encodes fibronectin, a secreted glycoprotein with no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no inner- or outer-leaflet lipidation. It exists as a soluble plasma dimer and as ECM-deposited multimers. The 'cell surface' form is pericellular ECM assembled extracellularly adjacent to cells, anchored via reversible non-covalent interactions with integrins, collagen, fibrin, and heparan sulfate proteoglycans — not stably tethered to the plasma membrane by any wash-resistant covalent or stable post-translational link to a TM partner. Applying all 5 contextual checks: (1) no cell-death/ICD-induced outer-leaflet display documented for FN1 protein body; (2) no narrow germline/tissue-restricted TM display; (3) not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) no documented minority PM pool with own anchor — the 'cell surface' residence is ECM/integrin-ligand equilibrium that washes off; (5) no wash-resistant stable TM-partner tethering. Anti-FN EDB clinical programs target tumor ECM/stroma fibronectin, not the plasma membrane surface of tumor cells, so they do not establish cell-surface accessibility. FN1 is secreted_only.
